title: Thick Hangover-Cure Fried Portuguese Pork Shoulder Paella
description: Fatty, shaggy, hangover-cure Portuguese dish featuring pork shoulder, air-fry, ovo-vegetarian-friendly, vessel=paella-pan.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the paella pan over medium-high heat.
Add the garlic and saut7e for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the pork shoulder and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the smoked paprika, chili flakes, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the potatoes and stir to combine.
Add the paella rice and stir to combine, coating the rice with the oil and the spices.
Add 2 cups of water and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes.
While the paella is cooking, heat the butter in a small pan over medium heat.
Add the green onions and saut7e until softened, about 2 minutes.
Fluff the paella with a fork and serve with the green onions and lemon wedges on top.
Air-fry the paella in the hot air fryer at 40076F for 10 minutes to crisp up the bottom.
Let the paella rest for 5 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld.
If the paella is too liquid, cook it for a few more minutes to thicken it up.
Adjust the seasoning with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve with additional lemon wedges on the side if desired.
